Title: Targeted mutagenesis of flavonoid biosynthesis pathway genes reveals functional divergence in seed coat colour, oil content and fatty acid composition in Brassica napus L.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MATE transporter with two MatE domains
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Mainly expressed in developing seeds; peaks around 22–28 DAF
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Mainly expressed in developing seeds; peaks around 22–28 DAF
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed storage energy and nutritional quality; proanthocyanidins contribute seed coat pigmentation and protection
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Oil in embryo/cotyledon oil bodies; PAs in seed coat
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed development stages 21–35 DAF under field-grown conditions
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout caused little visible seed coat color change but drastically reduced PA accumulation in seed coat, increased seed oil content the most among mutants (~8.25%), and shifted FA composition toward lower C18:1 and higher C18:2/C18:3
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CRISPR knockout of BnaTT12 strongly reduced PA accumulation in developing seed coat but mature seed color remained close to WT. 2. Bnatt12 showed the largest increase in seed oil content among all mutants. 3. Bnatt12 also showed altered FA composition with decreased oleic acid and increased linoleic and α-linolenic acids. 4. Oil body-related and lipid metabolic genes were up-regulated in the mutant.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Cas9 driven by 2×35S; sgRNAs driven by AtU3b/AtU3d/AtU6-1/AtU6-29
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Public RNA-seq database BnTIR used for tissue expression; qRT-PCR for targeted expression analysis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout of all functional copies; PA staining; oil/protein NIRS; FA profiling by GC; qRT-PCR; TEM of oil bodies
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phenotype uncouples PA staining and visible seed color, suggesting TT12 mainly affects PA transport rather than oxidation; vanillin/DMACA staining may not predict all yellow-seed materials
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant yield penalty; no serious defects in growth or seed development reported
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to raise oil content while maintaining yield; useful for yellow-seed/high-oil breeding, though FA composition shifts should be monitored

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavonoid B-ring substrates to produce 3'-hydroxylated flavonoids in the proanthocyanidin/flavonoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for PA/pigment biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Broadly expressed in multiple tissues, highest in developing seeds
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Broadly expressed in multiple tissues, highest in developing seeds
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: PAs determine seed coat pigmentation/protection; oil contributes to nutritional and agronomic value
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: PAs in seed coat; oil in embryo/cotyledon oil bodies
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed development stages 21–35 DAF under field conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout lightened seed coat, reduced PA and lignin accumulation, increased oil content by ~2–3%, and shifted FA composition toward lower C18:1 and higher C18:2/C18:3
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaTT7 knockout caused a visible lighter seed coat and reduced PA accumulation. 2. Mutation decreased lignin deposition and seed coat thickness. 3. Seed oil content increased moderately. 4. FA composition shifted toward more polyunsaturated fatty acids. 5. Flavonoid and lignin pathway genes were down-regulated while lipid genes were up-regulated.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Cas9 driven by 2×35S; sgRNAs driven by Arabidopsis U3/U6 promoters
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public RNA-seq database BnTIR; qRT-PCR expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; seed color phenotyping; PA staining; lignin assay; oil/protein NIRS; FA GC analysis; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Structural gene mutation also down-regulated regulatory genes BnaTT2 and BnaTT8, suggesting feedback regulation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant yield per plant reduction reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for yellow-seed and higher-oil breeding, but associated FA composition changes should be considered

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts leucoanthocyanidins to anthocyanidins in the late flavonoid/proanthocyanidin pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor for anthocyanin/PA synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Mainly expressed in developing seeds; peaks around 22–28 DAF
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Mainly expressed in developing seeds; peaks around 22–28 DAF
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed coat pigmentation/protection; oil contributes to nutritional value
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: PAs in seed coat; oil in embryo/cotyledon oil bodies
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed development stages 21–35 DAF under field conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout caused lighter seed coat, reduced PA and lignin accumulation, and increased oil content without significant FA composition change
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaTT18 knockout reduced seed coat pigmentation and PA accumulation. 2. It lowered lignin content and seed coat thickness. 3. It increased seed oil content while largely preserving FA composition. 4. This combination makes Bnatt18 an attractive breeding material.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Cas9 driven by 2×35S; sgRNAs driven by Arabidopsis U3/U6 promoters
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public RNA-seq database BnTIR; qRT-PCR expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; seed color and PA assays; lignin assay; oil/protein NIRS; FA GC; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors highlight Bnatt18 as an excellent starting material because oil increased without significant FA composition alteration
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for increasing oil content with minimal penalty on FA composition

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes and polymerizes proanthocyanidins during the terminal step of seed coat pigmentation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for modification/oxidation of target pigment rather than precursor formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant for visible dark seed coat pigmentation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Functional copies mainly expressed in developing seeds; gradually increase and peak at 35 DAF
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functional copies mainly expressed in developing seeds; gradually increase and peak at 35 DAF
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Oxidized proanthocyanidin-derived seed coat pigment; seed oil
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determines dark mature seed coat color; oil provides nutritional value
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed coat; oil in embryo/cotyledon oil bodies
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed maturation and storage affect visible phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout produced the most obvious yellow mature seeds despite near-WT PA accumulation during development, reduced lignin deposition, increased oil content modestly, lowered protein content, and altered FA composition toward lower C18:1 and higher C18:2/C18:3
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaTT10 knockout gave the strongest yellow-seed phenotype. 2. PA accumulation during development remained similar to WT, indicating BnaTT10 acts in PA oxidation/polymerization rather than PA synthesis. 3. Mature seed color darkened during storage. 4. Oil content increased modestly and FA composition shifted toward more C18:2/C18:3. 5. Protein content decreased significantly in this mutant.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Cas9 driven by 2×35S; sgRNAs driven by Arabidopsis U3/U6 promoters
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public RNA-seq database BnTIR; qRT-PCR expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; seed color observation including storage effect; PA staining; lignin assay; NIRS; FA GC; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phenotype uncouples developmental PA staining from final mature pigmentation; predicted BnaTT10.A02 lacked copper-binding domains and may be nonfunctional
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Protein content decreased significantly; no significant yield per plant penalty reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong yellow-seed trait source, but effects on protein content and postharvest color darkening require attention

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WIP zinc finger/SUF4-like domain containing C2H2 zinc finger
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Mainly expressed in developing seeds; low expression among BnaTT genes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Mainly expressed in developing seeds; low expression among BnaTT genes
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigmentation/protection of seed coat, structural reinforcement by lignin, and seed nutritional oil storage
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed coat for PAs/lignin; embryo/cotyledon oil bodies for oil
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Flavonoid and lignin biosynthesis; indirect control of oil accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation/synergistic regulation of flavonoid pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes expression of BnaTT4, BnaTT7, BnaTT10, BnaTT18, BnaBAN and BnaTT12; mutation reduces their expression
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: BnaTT4; BnaTT7; BnaTT10; BnaTT18; BnaBAN; BnaTT12
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts with BnaTT2/BnaTT8-like flavonoid regulatory system; Arabidopsis evidence suggests synergy with MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible feedback from structural gene perturbation affects regulator expression in pathway
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator of proper flavonoid pathway expression, but weaker than BnaTT2/BnaTT8 in this study
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout caused lighter seed coat, reduced PA and lignin accumulation, increased oil content modestly, and altered FA composition toward lower C18:1 and higher C18:2/C18:3
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaTT1 mutation caused a moderate yellow-seed phenotype and reduced seed coat lignification. 2. Flavonoid pathway genes were down-regulated, though less drastically than in Bnatt2 and Bnatt8. 3. Seed oil increased modestly and FA composition shifted toward more polyunsaturated FAs. 4. Function may be partially buffered by other WIP family members.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed development stages 21–35 DAF under field conditions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; seed color, PA staining, lignin assays, oil/protein NIRS, FA GC, qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Cas9 driven by 2×35S; sgRNAs driven by Arabidopsis U3/U6 promoters
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Public RNA-seq database BnTIR; qRT-PCR analysis
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors suggest partial restoration by WIP2/WIP3/WIP5/WIP6 may explain weaker phenotype versus BnaTT2/BnaTT8
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can contribute to lighter seed and higher oil, but weaker and potentially redundant target relative to BnaTT2/BnaTT8

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB transcription factor with two MYB domains
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Mainly expressed in developing seeds; low expression among BnaTT genes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Mainly expressed in developing seeds; low expression among BnaTT genes
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Proanthocyanidins; lignin; seed oil; fatty acid composition
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed coat pigmentation/protection, structural reinforcement, and seed oil quality/quantity
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed coat for PAs/lignin; embryo/cotyledon oil bodies for oil
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Flavonoid biosynthesis and indirect/possibly direct control of embryonic FA synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional regulation via MBW-like complex; repression of oil-promoting seed regulators inferred from mutant response
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates flavonoid structural genes and BnaTT12; negatively associated with BnaLEC1, BnaLEC2 and BnaFUS3 expression in mutants
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: BnaTT4; BnaTT7; BnaTT10; BnaTT18; BnaBAN; BnaTT12; BnaLEC1; BnaLEC2; BnaFUS3
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts synergistically with BnaTT8 and TTG1 in MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Structural gene mutations can reduce BnaTT2 expression, suggesting pathway feedback
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive regulator of PA/flavonoid deposition and major negative regulator of oil accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout produced near-Bnatt8 yellow seeds, reduced PA and lignin accumulation, increased oil content by about 5%, and shifted FA composition toward lower C18:1 and higher C18:2/C18:3
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaTT2 knockout strongly lightened seed coat and reduced PA accumulation. 2. It drastically down-regulated flavonoid pathway genes, similar to BnaTT8. 3. It increased seed oil content substantially without serious yield penalty. 4. It altered FA composition via associated up-regulation of BnaFAD2/BnaFAD3 and other lipid genes. 5. It likely acts together with BnaTT8/TTG1 as a key regulatory hub.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed development stages 21–35 DAF under field conditions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; seed color and PA assays; lignin assays; oil/protein NIRS; FA GC; qRT-PCR; TEM
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Cas9 driven by 2×35S; sgRNAs driven by Arabidopsis U3/U6 promoters
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Public RNA-seq database BnTIR; qRT-PCR expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional divergence from Arabidopsis noted because C18:1 decreases in rapeseed mutants but increases in Arabidopsis tt2
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: FA profile shifts away from oleic acid; no significant yield per plant penalty reported
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for yellow-seed/high-oil breeding, but FA composition effects require deployment with quality-improvement alleles if high oleic oil is desired

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bHLH transcription factor with bHLH-MYC_N and HLH domains
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Mainly expressed in developing seeds; low expression among BnaTT genes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Mainly expressed in developing seeds; low expression among BnaTT genes
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Proanthocyanidins; lignin; seed oil; fatty acid composition
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed coat color/protection and seed oil quantity/quality
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed coat for PAs/lignin; embryo/cotyledon oil bodies for oil
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Flavonoid biosynthesis and negative control of seed oil accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional regulation in MBW complex; represses oil-promoting developmental regulators based on mutant expression patterns
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates flavonoid structural genes and BnaTT12; negatively associated with BnaLEC1, BnaLEC2, BnaFUS3 and lipid synthesis genes in WT context
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: BnaTT4; BnaTT7; BnaTT10; BnaTT18; BnaBAN; BnaTT12; BnaLEC1; BnaLEC2; BnaFUS3
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Core component of MBW complex with BnaTT2 and TTG1
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Structural gene perturbations reduce BnaTT8 expression, consistent with feedback regulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential activator of PA deposition and strong negative regulator of seed oil accumulation; strongest effect on FA composition among mutants
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Previously generated knockout caused yellow seed coat, strong reduction in PA deposition, increased oil content by ~5%, and the most pronounced shift in FA composition among mutants
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaTT8 is a major regulator of seed coat color and PA deposition in rapeseed. 2. Its mutant shows strong down-regulation of flavonoid genes and transporter BnaTT12. 3. It strongly increases seed oil content and alters FA composition. 4. Mutant shows increased oil body number and reduced oil body size. 5. Works synergistically with BnaTT2 in the pathway.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed development stages 21–35 DAF under field conditions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior CRISPR/Cas9 mutant integrated with current phenotyping; seed color/PA assays; lignin assay; NIRS; FA GC; qRT-PCR; TEM
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR in this study; prior study data referenced
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core comparator mutant generated previously, not newly edited in this study; still central to Results and comparative conclusions
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: FA profile shifts may be undesirable for high-oleic breeding; no severe yield penalty reported
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Powerful target for yellow-seed/high-oil breeding, potentially best used with compensatory engineering of FA composition
